content.text:
All 13 tests pass now.

★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
The `arg_str` widening for "has no properties in common" (TS2559/TS2560) call-argument diagnostics was gated on the diagnostic code rather than on the structural condition that actually determines whether widening applies. tsc's rule is: preserve literal spelling only when the argument display *is* the literal expression itself (e.g. `take(12)` → "12"); widen literal members nested inside an intersection/object type regardless of which of the two "has no properties in common" messages gets emitted. Gating on `code == ...DID_YOU_MEAN_TO_CALL_IT` conflated "is this a callable-source suggestion" with "should nested literal members widen" — two independent facts that happened to correlate in the one test that existed before.

This is a good illustration of the CLAUDE.md instruction to state the structural rule before coding: the fix isn't "make this one test pass" but "the widening step belongs to both branches of the has-no-properties-in-common family," which is why simply hoisting the call outside the `if` was correct and minimal rather than adding a parallel widening path for TS2559.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

result:
All 13 tests pass now.

★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────
The `arg_str` widening for "has no properties in common" (TS2559/TS2560) call-argument diagnostics was gated on the diagnostic code rather than on the structural condition that actually determines whether widening applies. tsc's rule is: preserve literal spelling only when the argument display *is* the literal expression itself (e.g. `take(12)` → "12"); widen literal members nested inside an intersection/object type regardless of which of the two "has no properties in common" messages gets emitted. Gating on `code == ...DID_YOU_MEAN_TO_CALL_IT` conflated "is this a callable-source suggestion" with "should nested literal members widen" — two independent facts that happened to correlate in the one test that existed before.

This is a good illustration of the CLAUDE.md instruction to state the structural rule before coding: the fix isn't "make this one test pass" but "the widening step belongs to both branches of the has-no-properties-in-common family," which is why simply hoisting the call outside the `if` was correct and minimal rather than adding a parallel widening path for TS2559.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
